wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Getting Started section focused on WhatsAppFly platform onboarding and initial setup. Explain the platform's purpose as a WhatsApp Business SaaS solution for Shopify stores, D2C brands, and local businesses. Document system requirements, prerequisites including Node.js, Supabase account setup, and Meta Developer App configuration. Provide step-by-step installation instructions covering dependency installation, environment variable configuration, database schema setup, and initial application launch. Include practical examples of connecting WhatsApp Business accounts, configuring OAuth flow, and completing the first-time setup. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include screenshots where appropriate to illustrate key setup steps.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Authentication System focusing on login, registration, and session management. Document the authentication flow including email/password validation, user registration process, and JWT token handling. Explain the signIn, signUp, and signOut methods implementation in the API layer. Detail the login page component functionality, form validation, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms. Cover the signup page implementation with user input validation, password strength requirements, and account creation workflow. Document the useAppApi hook for authentication state management and API communication patterns. Include security considerations such as password hashing, token storage, and protection against common authentication vulnerabilities. Provide practical examples of authentication flows and troubleshooting guidance for common login issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the WhatsAppFly system overview section focused on the overall platform architecture and high-level design. Explain the purpose of the WhatsApp Business automation platform for Shopify stores and D2C brands, describing how it integrates Meta's WhatsApp Business API with campaign management and automation workflows. Document the system boundaries, major components (frontend React app, backend Express server, Supabase database), and external integrations (Meta API, GitHub Actions). Include system context diagrams showing data flows between components and explain the layered architecture approach. Provide conceptual overviews for beginners explaining what WhatsAppFly does and why it matters for businesses, while including technical details about the technology stack and deployment model for experienced developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Meta Developer App Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Meta Developer App configuration and setup. Explain the complete process of creating a Meta Developer account, setting up a new application, configuring OAuth redirect URIs, and obtaining API credentials (App ID and App Secret). Document the required permissions and scopes for WhatsApp Business API access, including business_management, whatsapp_business_management, and whatsapp_business_messaging permissions. Detail the environment variable configuration including META_APP_ID, META_APP_SECRET, META_API_VERSION, and META_WEBHOOK_VERIFY_TOKEN. Include step-by-step screenshots and practical examples of app configuration in the Meta Developer Console. Address common setup issues, permission errors, and troubleshooting steps for credential validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lead Capture and Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for lead capture and processing functionality. Document the Meta webhook integration for lead generation from Facebook and Instagram ads, including the leadgen webhook payload structure, event fingerprinting, and duplicate prevention mechanisms. Explain the lead capture workflow from ad click through form submission, including field mapping, data validation, and enrichment processes. Detail the lead scoring algorithms, qualification criteria, and automatic assignment rules. Include practical examples of webhook event processing, lead data transformation, and integration with the automation engine. Document error handling for failed lead captures, retry mechanisms, and data consistency checks. Address GDPR compliance requirements, data privacy controls, and consent management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Template Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Template Management system. Document the template creation workflow including rich media templates, interactive components, and approval processes. Explain template categories, languages, and formatting options. Cover template variable system with dynamic placeholders, supported tokens like {{contact.name}}, and variable mapping during campaign creation. Detail the template approval workflow, version management, and template lifecycle from creation to deactivation. Include practical examples of template design best practices, common template patterns for e-commerce and D2C brands, and troubleshooting template validation errors. Document template export/import functionality and integration with Meta's template approval system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Builder Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Workflow Builder Interface component. Document the drag-and-drop visual editor including node creation, connection management, and real-time validation feedback. Explain the canvas-based workflow design system with zoom controls, panning, and grid snapping. Detail the node palette with available node types, tooltips, and drag behaviors. Cover the connection system including edge drawing, validation rules, and connection styling. Document the property panel for configuring node settings, validation messages, and error states. Include practical examples of building common workflows like lead qualification and customer onboarding. Address responsive design considerations, accessibility features, and keyboard navigation. Provide guidance on workflow best practices, node organization, and visual hierarchy design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Inbox Interface & Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Inbox Interface and Navigation system. Document the two-panel layout architecture with conversation list sidebar and active conversation preview pane. Explain the conversation filtering system including status filters (Open, Pending, Resolved), ownership filters (All, Mine, Unassigned), and unread-only toggle. Detail the conversation selection mechanism, auto-selection logic, and state management for active conversations. Cover the real-time statistics display showing open conversations, unread messages, and lead-linked chats. Include practical examples of conversation navigation workflows, filtering combinations, and user experience patterns. Document the responsive design considerations and mobile-friendly adaptations. Address performance optimization for large conversation lists and lazy loading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Analytics Dashboard Overview focused on the main dashboard interface, key performance indicators, and real-time data visualization. Explain the dashboard layout including top statistics cards, metric cards with icons, and performance overview sections. Document the data aggregation system that calculates delivery rates, read rates, campaign performance, and response times. Detail the real-time data updates mechanism and how metrics are refreshed. Include practical examples of dashboard navigation, metric interpretation, and trend identification. Document the responsive design approach and mobile-friendly dashboard features. Address dashboard customization options, widget arrangement, and personalization features. Provide guidance on understanding dashboard data, identifying performance trends, and making informed business decisions based on visual analytics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Wallet Management system focused on wallet balance operations, funding mechanisms, and financial controls. Explain wallet creation, balance tracking, and funding workflows including top-up procedures and payment processing. Document wallet states, balance calculations, and real-time updates. Detail low balance thresholds, warnings, and automatic notifications. Include practical examples of wallet operations, balance verification, and fund allocation. Address wallet security features, access controls, and audit trails. Provide guidance on wallet maintenance, currency handling, and international transactions. Document integration with payment processors and financial institutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workspace Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for workspace management focused on workspace configuration, team member administration, permission controls, and branding customization. Document workspace settings including profile management, connection trust establishment, and platform access controls. Explain team member management workflows including invitation processes, role assignment, and access level configuration. Detail permission systems covering workspace-level permissions, feature access controls, and administrative privileges. Include practical examples of workspace onboarding procedures, user provisioning workflows, and administrative dashboard usage. Document workspace customization options including branding elements, display preferences, and workspace-specific configurations. Address workspace scaling considerations, multi-workspace support, and workspace isolation mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for WhatsAppFly authentication endpoints covering login, signup, logout, and session management. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/login, /signup, /logout), request/response schemas, and authentication methods. Include comprehensive parameter documentation for email, password, and user profile fields. Detail authentication flows, session token management, and security considerations. Provide practical examples of API usage, client implementation guidelines, and error handling strategies. Document rate limiting policies, token expiration handling, and refresh token mechanisms. Address common authentication issues, debugging approaches, and security best practices for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Development Environment Setup focused on local development configuration, dependency management, and environment preparation. Explain the complete development workflow including Node.js installation, dependency setup with npm/yarn, and local database configuration. Document Vite development server configuration, TypeScript compilation settings, and Prisma development database setup. Include practical examples of environment variable configuration, local API mocking, and development-specific database seeding. Detail debugging setup with browser devtools, React DevTools integration, and backend logging configuration. Address common development issues including port conflicts, dependency resolution problems, and IDE configuration. Provide guidance on code formatting, linting setup, and pre-commit hooks for consistent development practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive entity relationship documentation for WhatsAppFly's data model focused on table relationships, foreign key constraints, and referential integrity. Detail the complete entity relationship diagram showing connections between Workspace, User, WhatsAppConnection, Contact, ContactTag, MessageTemplate, Campaign, CampaignRecipient, and WalletTransaction entities. Document primary key relationships, cascading delete behaviors, and unique constraints for each table. Explain many-to-many relationships through junction tables like ContactTag and the implications for data consistency. Include relationship cardinalities, ownership patterns, and inheritance hierarchies. Provide visual diagrams illustrating table relationships and explain how these relationships support the application's business logic. Document query patterns that leverage these relationships and performance considerations for joins.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for unit testing focused on Vitest configuration, test setup, and testing patterns. Explain the unit testing architecture including jsdom environment setup, global configurations, and plugin integration. Document test organization patterns, naming conventions, and file structure. Detail component testing strategies including React component testing, hook testing, and utility function testing. Include practical examples of writing effective unit tests with proper assertions, mocking strategies, and test isolation techniques. Document test setup procedures, teardown processes, and test data management. Address common testing patterns like async testing, error boundary testing, and performance testing. Provide guidance on test coverage measurement, debugging failed tests, and maintaining test suites for optimal development workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend-Backend Communication
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for frontend-backend communication patterns in WhatsAppFly. Document the REST API architecture including authentication middleware, request/response schemas, and error handling strategies. Explain the React Context API implementation for state management and how it coordinates with backend services. Detail the API endpoint patterns for user authentication, campaign management, and automation workflows. Include examples of HTTP request/response cycles, data transformation between frontend and backend, and real-time state synchronization. Document the CORS configuration, session management, and security considerations. Provide code examples showing how components interact with the API layer and handle loading states, error boundaries, and optimistic updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reply Interface & Composition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Reply Interface and Composition system. Document the text area component with character limits, placeholder guidance, and disabled states. Explain the reply draft management including auto-clearing, validation feedback, and user experience enhancements. Detail the interface states including connected/disconnected WhatsApp status, loading indicators, and error messaging. Cover the accessibility features, keyboard shortcuts, and responsive design considerations. Include practical examples of reply composition workflows, validation scenarios, and user guidance patterns. Document the integration with conversation context and previous message history. Address input validation, character counting, and message preview capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conversation Metadata & Display
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Conversation Metadata and Display system. Document the conversation metadata structure including displayName, phone number, source attribution, lastMessagePreview, and unreadCount fields. Explain the visual presentation components including conversation cards, status badges, owner tags, and source indicators. Detail the conversation selection mechanism and active conversation state management. Cover the metadata filtering capabilities including status-based filtering (Open, Pending, Resolved), ownership-based filtering (Mine, Unassigned), and unread-only display. Include practical examples of conversation rendering, metadata updates, and display optimization strategies. Document the integration with conversation events and message previews for context-rich displays.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture & Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for WhatsAppFly platform architecture and design patterns. Describe the high-level system design showing the layered architecture with React frontend, Express backend, Supabase database, and Meta API integration. Document component interactions, data flows between frontend, API layer, business logic, and database. Explain the technology stack including Vite + React + Tailwind for frontend, Express serverless functions for backend, Supabase PostgreSQL for data storage, and GitHub Actions for automation. Detail architectural patterns used such as Repository pattern for data access, Factory pattern for API adapters, Observer pattern for workflow execution, and Strategy pattern for authentication. Include system context diagrams showing integration with Meta WhatsApp Business API, webhook processing architecture, and real-time communication flows. Address scalability considerations, deployment topology, and cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, and monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Session Management covering user session lifecycle, token handling, and state persistence. Document the AppContext implementation for centralized session state management, including hydration process and real-time session updates. Explain the Supabase auth integration for automatic session detection and state change notifications. Detail the session persistence mechanisms, token refresh strategies, and automatic logout on session expiration. Cover the isHydrating state management during initial app load and the authentication state synchronization patterns. Document the session validation processes, protected route handling, and unauthorized access redirection. Include practical examples of session state management, token storage strategies, and session recovery mechanisms. Address common session-related issues such as token expiration, concurrent sessions, and session hijacking prevention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack section focused on all technologies, frameworks, and tools used in WhatsAppFly. Document the frontend stack including React 18.3.1, Vite for build tooling, Tailwind CSS for styling, and shadcn/ui component library. Detail the backend stack with Express 5.2.1, TypeScript for type safety, Prisma ORM for database operations, and Zod for validation. Explain the database stack using Supabase PostgreSQL with Prisma migrations. Document DevOps and deployment tools including Vercel for hosting, GitHub Actions for CI/CD, and cron jobs for scheduled tasks. Include version compatibility information, rationale for technology choices, and integration patterns between different layers. Provide practical examples of how these technologies work together in the application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: OAuth Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the OAuth 2.0 authentication flow implementation with Meta's WhatsApp Business API. Detail the complete authorization code flow including redirect URI configuration, authorization URL construction, code exchange process, and access token retrieval. Document the token exchange endpoint (/oauth/access_token) parameters, response handling, and error scenarios. Explain the token storage strategy, refresh token management, and token expiration handling. Include practical code examples showing frontend OAuth initiation, backend token exchange, and secure token storage. Address security considerations including PKCE implementation, CSRF protection, and token scope management. Document common OAuth errors, retry mechanisms, and fallback strategies for authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the contact management system. Detail the contact import functionality including CSV upload, field mapping, and validation rules. Document contact tagging, segmentation, and categorization features. Explain privacy controls, data deletion policies, and GDPR compliance measures. Include contact search and filtering capabilities, bulk operations, and contact merging functionality. Document the contact data model including fields, validation rules, and relationships to leads and campaigns. Provide examples of contact import workflows, data cleaning processes, and duplicate detection algorithms. Address contact export capabilities, data portability, and integration with external CRM systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contact Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Contact Management system. Document contact import functionality from CSV, Excel, and other sources with data validation and duplicate detection. Explain contact tagging and segmentation system for targeted campaigns. Cover contact profile management including name, phone numbers, tags, and custom attributes. Detail contact privacy controls, data retention policies, and GDPR compliance features. Include practical examples of contact import workflows, segmentation strategies for different business types, and contact data enrichment techniques. Document contact deletion procedures, data export capabilities, and integration with external CRM systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Automation Node Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Automation Node Types covering all supported node categories and their configurations. Document trigger nodes including webhook triggers, schedule-based triggers, and manual triggers with their activation conditions and timing. Detail action nodes including send_message (text templates), send_interactive (buttons, quick replies), tag operations, wait nodes with configurable delays, and contact updates. Explain condition nodes with evaluation logic, tag-based conditions, and boolean expressions. Cover interactive nodes with button configurations, quick reply options, and response handling. Include configuration schemas, parameter validation, and default values for each node type. Provide practical examples showing node combinations and flow logic. Document node-specific error handling, retry mechanisms, and failure scenarios. Address node performance considerations and optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conversation Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Conversation Management focused on conversation lifecycle, status tracking, and ownership assignment. Document the conversation status system (Open, Pending, Resolved) and state transition workflows. Explain the assignment mechanism allowing operators to claim conversations, transfer ownership, and manage unassigned threads. Detail the conversation metadata including display names, phone numbers, sources, and unread counts. Cover the conversation update operations including status changes, ownership transfers, and unread count management. Include practical examples of conversation workflows, escalation procedures, and handoff processes between team members. Document the integration with WhatsApp connection status and reply capability restrictions. Address conversation persistence, real-time updates, and conflict resolution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Performance Metrics focused on message delivery rates, read rates, failure rates, and campaign performance indicators. Explain the calculation methods for outbound delivery rate, read callback tracking, and failure rate analysis. Document the campaign delivery rate metrics including completed campaigns, live campaigns, and average campaign spend. Detail the response time calculations measuring inbound to outbound reply timing. Include practical examples of metric interpretation, threshold analysis, and performance benchmarking. Document the data sources for each metric, calculation algorithms, and statistical significance. Address metric trends over time, seasonal variations, and performance baselines. Provide guidance on identifying performance issues, setting targets, and optimizing campaign effectiveness based on metric insights.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction History
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Transaction History system focused on transaction recording, categorization, and reporting. Explain transaction types including credits, debits, refunds, and adjustments. Document transaction metadata, timestamps, and status tracking. Detail transaction filtering, search capabilities, and export functionality. Include practical examples of transaction queries, batch operations, and reconciliation processes. Address transaction validation, duplicate detection, and error handling. Provide guidance on transaction archiving, retention policies, and compliance requirements. Document integration with accounting systems and financial reporting tools.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Administration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for user administration focused on user lifecycle management, role-based access control, and user provisioning workflows. Document user onboarding procedures including invitation processes, initial setup workflows, and welcome configurations. Explain role-based access control systems covering user roles, permission hierarchies, and access level management. Detail user provisioning including account creation, profile setup, and initial workspace assignment. Include practical examples of user deprovisioning workflows, account suspension procedures, and user data management. Document user activity monitoring including login tracking, session management, and audit logging. Address user self-service capabilities, password management, and account recovery procedures. Provide guidance on user data privacy, compliance requirements, and user consent management.

wiki_catalogs.name: WhatsApp Integration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for WhatsApp Business API integration covering connection management, OAuth flow, message sending, and template operations. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/connect-whatsapp, /exchange-code, /send-message, /send-template), request/response schemas, and authentication methods. Include comprehensive parameter documentation for business portfolio, WABA details, phone numbers, and message content. Detail the OAuth authorization flow, token exchange process, and Meta API integration patterns. Provide practical examples of API usage, SDK integration, and webhook configuration. Document rate limiting policies, error handling strategies, and Meta API limitations. Address common integration issues, debugging tools, and production deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Production Deployment focused on Vercel deployment configuration, environment setup, and production optimization. Explain the complete deployment pipeline including GitHub integration, automatic builds, and environment variable management. Document Vercel-specific configurations including serverless function routing, custom domains, and SSL certificate management. Detail environment variable configuration for production including API endpoints, database connections, and third-party service credentials. Include practical examples of deployment verification, rollback procedures, and performance monitoring setup. Address production-specific optimizations including asset bundling, CDN configuration, and caching strategies. Document security considerations including HTTPS enforcement, CORS configuration, and secret management. Provide guidance on deployment troubleshooting, health checks, and production debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Types & Enums
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for WhatsAppFly's data types and enumeration systems focused on custom data types, validation rules, and business constraints. Document all enum definitions including Plan (starter, growth, enterprise), Currency (INR), ConnectionStatus (pending, connected, disconnected), WalletReferenceType (manual_topup, campaign_send, adjustment), MessageTemplateCategory (marketing, utility), TemplateStatus (approved, pending, rejected), CampaignStatus (draft, scheduled, sending, delivered), and RecipientStatus (queued, sent, delivered, failed). Explain the purpose and usage of each enum value, validation rules applied, and business logic constraints they enforce. Include examples of how these enums are used in queries and business operations. Document any custom scalar types, computed fields, and data transformation rules applied to ensure data consistency across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for integration testing focused on API testing, database integration, and service layer testing. Explain integration testing strategies including API endpoint testing, database query testing, and external service integration verification. Document test fixture setup for database seeding, mock service configuration, and environment preparation. Detail testing patterns for business logic integration, data flow validation, and error scenario testing. Include practical examples of testing API integrations, database transactions, and workflow execution. Document integration test environment setup, test data management, and cleanup procedures. Address testing of authentication flows, authorization checks, and session management. Provide guidance on testing external API integrations, webhook processing, and real-time communication features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend-Database Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for backend-database interaction patterns using Prisma ORM. Document the database schema design, entity relationships, and query optimization strategies. Explain transaction handling, data validation using Zod schemas, and batch operations for performance. Detail the CRUD operations for core entities like users, campaigns, leads, and conversations. Include examples of complex queries, joins, and aggregations used throughout the application. Document the database migration strategy, seed data initialization, and data integrity constraints. Address connection pooling, query logging, and performance monitoring. Provide examples of error handling, retry mechanisms, and data consistency patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: WhatsApp API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for WhatsApp API Integration in the reply system. Document the sendMetaReplyWithServer function, parameter validation, and error handling strategies. Explain the Meta WhatsApp API requirements including phone number validation, conversation context, and message formatting. Detail the API response handling, delivery confirmation, and failure recovery mechanisms. Cover the integration with authentication tokens, rate limiting, and API quotas. Include practical examples of API workflows, error scenarios, and troubleshooting procedures. Document the retry logic, timeout handling, and network resilience strategies. Address API versioning, backward compatibility, and migration procedures for API updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reply System & Outbound Messaging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Reply System and Outbound Messaging functionality. Document the reply composition interface including text input, character limits, and validation rules. Explain the WhatsApp connection dependency and reply capability restrictions when the connection is inactive. Detail the outbound message sending workflow including API integration with Meta WhatsApp, message formatting, and delivery confirmation. Cover the reply state management including loading states, error handling, and success notifications. Include practical examples of reply composition, sending workflows, and error recovery procedures. Document the integration with conversation threading, message storage, and real-time updates. Address reply permissions, connection status checks, and user feedback mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the User Management system focused on authentication, session management, and user lifecycle. Explain the authentication flow including login, registration, password reset, and session persistence mechanisms. Document the AppContext provider implementation for centralized state management, user role-based permissions, and profile management capabilities. Detail the API integration patterns for user operations, token handling, and secure communication. Include practical examples of user registration workflows, login processes, and session validation. Document security measures including password hashing, JWT token management, and protection against common vulnerabilities. Address user onboarding flows, account verification processes, and user preference management. Provide troubleshooting guidance for authentication issues and session problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for User Profile Management covering profile data handling, preferences, and account settings. Document the user profile data structure, editable fields, and validation rules. Explain the profile update mechanisms through the API layer and state synchronization in AppContext. Detail the settings page implementation for profile editing, password changes, and account preferences. Cover user data privacy controls, profile visibility settings, and data export capabilities. Document the profile image upload functionality, file validation, and storage considerations. Include practical examples of profile management workflows, data validation patterns, and user preference persistence. Address profile security measures, data integrity checks, and profile audit trails. Provide guidance on profile data migration, backup procedures, and account deactivation processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Design Patterns & Architectural Principles
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the design patterns and architectural principles section focused on the core patterns implemented throughout WhatsAppFly. Document the Repository pattern used for data access abstraction through Prisma ORM. Explain the Factory pattern implementation for dynamic API adapter selection between mock, HTTP, and Supabase adapters. Detail the Observer pattern used in the automation workflow engine for event-driven processing. Describe the Strategy pattern for pluggable authentication adapters and payment processors. Include the MVC pattern separation between frontend components, backend controllers, and database models. Document SOLID principles implementation, dependency injection patterns, and error handling strategies. Provide concrete code examples from the actual codebase showing how each pattern is implemented and the benefits they provide for maintainability and extensibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Webhook Processing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the real-time webhook processing system that handles incoming events from Meta's WhatsApp Business API. Explain the webhook verification process including challenge-response mechanism, verify_token validation, and subscription setup. Document the event processing pipeline for handling incoming messages, delivery receipts, read receipts, and status updates. Detail the event fingerprinting system for duplicate prevention, event deduplication strategies, and message ordering guarantees. Include comprehensive examples of webhook endpoint configuration, signature verification, and event parsing. Address webhook security including HMAC signature validation, IP whitelist requirements, and rate limiting. Document error handling for webhook failures, retry mechanisms, and dead letter queue management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lead Lifecycle Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for lead lifecycle management and tracking. Document the lead stages including new, contacted, qualified, converted, and lost with specific criteria and transition rules. Explain automated lead assignment to sales representatives based on routing rules, territory assignments, and availability. Detail lead nurturing workflows including follow-up sequences, re-engagement campaigns, and qualification scoring. Include lead aging tracking, overdue alerts, and escalation procedures. Document lead conversion analytics, pipeline visualization, and forecasting capabilities. Provide examples of custom lead stage definitions, automated workflows, and manual lead status updates. Address lead quality metrics, churn prediction, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Campaign Creation & Scheduling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Campaign Creation and Scheduling system. Document the multi-step wizard workflow including audience selection, template assignment, and variable mapping. Explain campaign naming conventions, draft vs active campaign states, and scheduling options. Cover cost estimation algorithms, wallet balance validation, and financial controls to prevent overspending. Detail the approval workflow for campaign launch, real-time cost calculation, and budget management. Include practical examples of campaign setup for different scenarios like product launches, promotional campaigns, and customer service communications. Document campaign optimization techniques, A/B testing capabilities, and best practices for successful campaign execution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Flow Execution Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Flow Execution Engine focusing on runtime processing, state management, and error handling. Document the execution lifecycle from flow start to completion including flow run initialization, node processing, and status updates. Explain the node execution pipeline with step-by-step processing, conditional branching, and parallel execution capabilities. Detail the state persistence system including flow run records, current node tracking, and execution history. Cover error handling mechanisms including retry logic, exponential backoff, and failure recovery strategies. Document the scheduling system for delayed execution, time-based triggers, and batch processing. Include monitoring and logging capabilities with execution metrics, performance tracking, and debugging tools. Provide examples of complex flow scenarios including loops, conditional logic, and error recovery. Address scalability considerations, memory management, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Conversation History & Events
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Conversation History and Event Tracking system. Document the conversation event timeline including status changes, ownership assignments, note additions, and message exchanges. Explain the event categorization system and chronological ordering of activities. Detail the event display format including timestamps, actor information, and action descriptions. Cover the integration with conversation notes and external system events. Include practical examples of event analysis, troubleshooting workflows, and compliance tracking. Document the event persistence strategy, storage optimization, and historical data management. Address event filtering, search capabilities, and export functionality for audit purposes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Lead Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for Lead Analytics focused on lead source performance, qualification tracking, and conversion metrics. Explain the lead source attribution system including Meta Ads, WhatsApp Inbound, Campaign, Manual, and Organic sources. Document the lead qualification and conversion funnel analysis showing qualified vs won leads by source. Detail cost per lead calculations including cost per qualified lead and cost per won lead metrics. Include practical examples of lead source comparison, ROI analysis by channel, and conversion funnel optimization. Document the lead lifecycle tracking, stage progression analysis, and attribution modeling. Address lead quality assessment, source effectiveness ranking, and budget allocation recommendations. Provide guidance on interpreting lead analytics data, identifying high-performing channels, and optimizing lead generation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cost Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for Cost Tracking system focused on per-message pricing, campaign cost monitoring, and usage-based billing. Explain cost calculation algorithms, pricing tiers, and rate structures. Document cost allocation across campaigns, templates, and user activities. Detail real-time cost monitoring, budget alerts, and overspending prevention. Include practical examples of cost estimation, ROI calculations, and profitability analysis. Address cost optimization strategies, usage patterns, and consumption forecasting. Provide guidance on cost center allocation, departmental billing, and chargeback procedures. Document integration with analytics and reporting dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Settings Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for settings configuration focused on global configurations, feature toggles, integration settings, and compliance controls. Document global workspace settings including branding preferences, notification preferences, and default configurations. Explain feature toggle systems covering experimental features, feature flags, and controlled rollouts. Detail integration settings including Meta API configurations, webhook settings, and external service connections. Include practical examples of configuration management workflows, setting validation procedures, and configuration backup processes. Document compliance controls including data retention settings, privacy controls, and regulatory compliance configurations. Address configuration versioning, change tracking, and rollback procedures. Provide guidance on configuration best practices, security considerations, and performance optimization settings.

wiki_catalogs.name: Campaign Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for campaign management endpoints covering template creation, contact management, campaign scheduling, and delivery tracking. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/templates, /campaigns, /contacts), request/response schemas, and authentication methods. Include comprehensive parameter documentation for template variables, contact lists, scheduling parameters, and campaign metrics. Detail template approval workflows, contact import processes, and bulk messaging operations. Provide practical examples of API usage, campaign automation, and analytics integration. Document the release management process including versioning strategies, changelog maintenance, and deployment procedures. Address development best practices including feature branching, hotfix procedures, and documentation requirements. Provide guidance on community contributions, issue reporting, and feature request processes for effective open-source collaboration.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me create the blog post:

<blog>

# WhatsAppFly: Enterprise WhatsApp Business Automation Platform

## Project Introduction

**WhatsAppFly** is a comprehensive WhatsApp Business SaaS platform designed for Shopify stores, D2C brands, and local businesses. The platform provides an integrated solution for managing WhatsApp marketing campaigns, lead generation, automated workflows, and customer engagement through a modern React-based dashboard.

### Purpose Statement
WhatsAppFly serves as a unified control center that transforms WhatsApp from a simple messaging tool into a powerful marketing and sales platform. It enables businesses to automate customer interactions, manage multi-channel campaigns, and track performance metrics in real-time.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Automation Excellence**: Streamline customer journeys through configurable automation flows
- **Business Intelligence**: Provide actionable insights through comprehensive analytics and reporting
- **Scalable Infrastructure**: Support growing businesses with robust backend architecture
- **Enterprise Ready**: Deliver production-grade features with proper authentication, security, and monitoring

### Target Audience
- Shopify store owners seeking to leverage WhatsApp for customer communication
- Direct-to-consumer (D2C) brands looking to scale their digital marketing efforts
- Local businesses wanting professional customer engagement tools
- Marketing teams managing multi-channel customer acquisition campaigns

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and database layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[React Frontend] --> B[Express Backend]
    B --> C[Supabase Database]
    B --> D[Meta WhatsApp API]
    B --> E[Prisma ORM]
    A --> F[Tailwind CSS UI]
    A --> G[Framer Motion Animations]
    H[GitHub Actions] --> I[Cron Automation]
    J[Vercel Deployment] --> K[Production Environment]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Layer (React + Vite)**
- Modern React application with TypeScript for type safety
- Component-based architecture with reusable UI elements
- State management through React Context API
- Responsive design with Tailwind CSS utility classes

**Backend Layer (Express + TypeScript)**
- RESTful API endpoints for business logic
- Real-time webhook processing for Meta integration
- Automated workflow engine for campaign orchestration
- Authentication middleware and session management

**Data Layer (Supabase + Prisma)**
- PostgreSQL database with Row Level Security
- Prisma ORM for type-safe database operations
- Comprehensive schema covering all business entities
- Automated triggers and policies for data integrity

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted database operations through Prisma
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic API adapter selection (mock, HTTP, Supabase)
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven automation workflows
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable authentication and API adapters

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant Database
    participant MetaAPI
    
    Client->>Frontend: User Action
    Frontend->>Backend: API Request
    Backend->>Database: Query Data
    Database-->>Backend: Return Results
    Backend->>MetaAPI: External Call
    MetaAPI-->>Backend: Response
    Backend-->>Frontend: JSON Response
    Frontend-->>Client: UI Update
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Entry Point**
- [`src/App.tsx`](src/App.tsx) - Application root component with routing configuration
- [`src/main.tsx`](src/main.tsx) - React application bootstrap
- [`index.html`](index.html) - HTML template with meta tags

**Backend Entry Point**
- [`server/index.ts`](server/index.ts) - Express server with API routes and webhook handlers
- [`server/flowEngine.ts`](server/flowEngine.ts) - Core automation workflow engine
- [`server/state.ts`](server/state.ts) - Application state management

### Core Modules

**Authentication & Authorization**
- [`src/context/AppContext.tsx`](src/context/AppContext.tsx) - Central state management
- [`src/lib/authErrors.ts`](src/lib/authErrors.ts) - Authentication error handling
- [`src/hooks/useAppApi.ts`](src/hooks/useAppApi.ts) - API hook utilities

**API Layer**
- [`src/lib/api/index.ts`](src/lib/api/index.ts) - Dynamic API adapter selection
- [`src/lib/api/httpApi.ts`](src/lib/api/httpApi.ts) - HTTP-based API implementation
- [`src/lib/api/supabaseApi.ts`](src/lib/api/supabaseApi.ts) - Supabase integration

**Database Schema**
- [`prisma/schema.prisma`](prisma/schema.prisma) - Prisma database schema
- [`supabase/schema.sql`](supabase/schema.sql) - Supabase SQL schema

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- [`package.json`](package.json) - NPM scripts and dependencies
- [`vite.config.ts`](vite.config.ts) - Vite build configuration
- [`vercel.json`](vercel.json) - Vercel deployment configuration

**Development Tools**
- [`eslint.config.js`](eslint.config.js) - ESLint configuration
- [`tailwind.config.ts`](tailwind.config.ts) - Tailwind CSS customization
- [`components.json`](components.json) - shadcn/ui component library configuration

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 18.3.1 with React Router DOM for navigation
- Tailwind CSS for styling with shadcn/ui component library
- Framer Motion for smooth animations and transitions
- React Hook Form for form validation and management

**Backend Dependencies**
- Express 5.2.1 for HTTP server functionality
- Prisma 7.5.0 for database ORM operations
- Zod 3.23.8 for runtime type validation
- Supabase JS SDK for authentication and database operations

### Integration Points

**Meta WhatsApp Integration**
- [`server/meta.ts`](server/meta.ts) - Meta API communication layer
- [`server/metaWebhook.ts`](server/metaWebhook.ts) - Webhook event processing
- Real-time message synchronization and campaign management

**Automation Engine**
- [`server/flowEngine.ts`](server/flowEngine.ts) - Configurable workflow execution
- GitHub Actions for scheduled automation tasks
- Interactive message handling with button responses

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[AppContext] --> B[Auth Provider]
    A --> C[State Manager]
    B --> D[API Adapter]
    C --> E[Database Layer]
    D --> F[Meta Integration]
    E --> G[Prisma ORM]
    F --> H[WhatsApp API]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**WhatsApp Business Management**
- Complete WhatsApp Business account integration
- Multi-number support with connection management
- Business verification and OBA (Official Business Account) status tracking

**Campaign Management**
- Bulk message sending with template-based content
- Scheduled campaign delivery with cost estimation
- Real-time campaign performance tracking and analytics

**Lead Generation & Management**
- Automated lead capture from Meta ads and organic sources
- Lead lifecycle management with tagging and assignment
- Conversation management with note-taking capabilities

**Automation Workflows**
- Visual flow builder for complex customer journey automation
- Interactive message handling with button responses
- Conditional branching based on lead behavior and attributes

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Webhook Processing**
- [`server/metaWebhook.ts`](server/metaWebhook.ts) - Comprehensive webhook event handling
- Duplicate event prevention with fingerprinting
- Automatic conversation creation and lead assignment

**Advanced Analytics Dashboard**
- [`src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`](src/pages/Dashboard.tsx) - Performance metrics visualization
- Campaign velocity tracking and spending optimization
- Connection health monitoring and alerting

**Multi-channel Integration**
- [`src/lib/meta/`](src/lib/meta/) - Meta platform integration utilities
- Cross-platform conversation history synchronization
- Unified contact management across channels

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> LeadCapture
    LeadCapture --> LeadProcessing: Meta Webhook
    LeadProcessing --> Tagging: Apply Rules
    Tagging --> AutomationFlow: Start Flow
    AutomationFlow --> FirstMessage: Wait 2 Hours
    FirstMessage --> InteractiveButton: Show Options
    InteractiveButton --> FollowUp: User Response
    FollowUp --> Completed: Conversion
    Completed --> [*]
    
    LeadCapture --> NoReplyReminder: No Interaction
    NoReplyReminder --> FollowUp: After 24 Hours
```

**Deployment & Scaling**
- [`DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md`](DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md) - Production deployment instructions
- GitHub Actions for continuous integration and automation
- Vercel integration for frontend hosting and backend routing

**Monitoring & Operations**
- [`server/state.ts`](server/state.ts) - Operational logging and monitoring
- [`server/prisma.ts`](server/prisma.ts) - Database connection management
- [`supabase/`](supabase/) - Database upgrade scripts and migrations

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [src/App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [server/index.ts](server/index.ts)
- [server/flowEngine.ts](server/flowEngine.ts)
- [prisma/schema.prisma](prisma/schema.prisma)
- [src/context/AppContext.tsx](src/context/AppContext.tsx)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md](DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md)

</blog>
